title: Feature Implementation Guide — NXGN Actions Web
purpose: **AI Summary**: Step-by-step guide for implementing a frontend feature in the Actions Angular app. Covers TypeScript models, feature service, list page, capture page, capture state (for aggregates with child routes), embedded lists, routing, and validation. Includes a runtime data-flow narrative showing how user actions flow through each layer, and a decision tree for choosing which pieces a feature needs.

examples.name: How Data Flows at Runtime Example 1
examples.code:
User navigates to /admin/workflows
  │
  ▼
Router activates WorkflowListComponent           ← standalone, OnPush, lazy-loaded
  │ ngOnInit()
  ▼
workflowService.getGridList(page, pageSize)      ← feature-colocated HTTP service
  │ POST /api/v1/{entities}/grid-list
  ▼
API returns GridListResponse<GridItemDTO>
  │
  ▼
Component sets signals:
  │ dataItems.set(response.items)
  │ totalCount.set(response.totalCount)
  │ loading.set(false)
  ▼
Template renders:
  │ nxgn-grid-page-title (toolbar: Add, Edit, Delete)
  │ nxgn-data-grid (rows bound to dataItems(), columns to columnDefs)
  │ Footer shows totalCount()

examples.name: How Data Flows at Runtime Example 2
examples.code:
User clicks [+ Add] on list page
  │
  ▼
Router navigates to /admin/workflows/new
  │ no :id param → isEditMode = false
  ▼
WorkflowCaptureComponent activates
  │ Form signals initialized to defaults (empty strings, null selections)
  │ Lookup data loaded (enum options for dropdowns)
  ▼
User fills in form fields
  │ Signals update reactively
  │ Validation computed() functions recalculate in real-time
  │ Slug auto-generated from Name (create mode only, stops on manual edit)
  ▼
User clicks [Save]
  │ isFormValid() checked → must be true
  ▼
Build SaveRequest from signals:
  │ id: EMPTY_GUID (signals create)
  │ All form fields mapped to request shape
  │ Child collections: temp UUIDs → EMPTY_GUID for new items
  ▼
workflowService.save(request)
  │ POST /api/v1/workflows
  ▼
API returns SaveResponse { id }
  │
  ▼
Router navigates to /admin/workflows/{id}        ← switches to edit mode
  │ toastr.success('Created')

examples.name: How Data Flows at Runtime Example 3
examples.code:
User double-clicks row on list page (or clicks Edit)
  │
  ▼
Router navigates to /admin/workflows/{id}
  │ :id param = valid GUID → isEditMode = true
  ▼
effect() fires on id change:
  │ loading.set(true)
  ▼
workflowService.get(id)
  │ GET /api/v1/workflows/{id}
  ▼
API returns WorkflowDTO (full entity with children)
  │
  ▼
Populate form signals from DTO:
  │ name.set(dto.name), slug.set(dto.slug), ...
  │ state.steps.set(dto.steps)         ← capture state for child routes
  │ serverMemberIds = new Set(dto.steps.map(m => m.id))
  │ loading.set(false)
  ▼
User edits fields → validation recomputes
  ▼
User clicks [Save]
  │ Build SaveRequest (id = entity.id, steps mapped with ID management)
  ▼
workflowService.save(request)
  │ POST /api/v1/workflows → 200 OK
  ▼
Reload entity data (re-fetch GET to sync server state)
  │ toastr.success('Updated')

examples.name: How Data Flows at Runtime Example 4
examples.code:
User selects rows on list page → clicks [Delete]
  │
  ▼
confirmService.confirmDelete(title, message)          ← modal confirmation
  │ user confirms
  ▼
workflowService.delete({ entityKeys: [...ids] })
  │ POST /api/v1/workflows/delete → 204
  ▼
toastr.success('Deleted')
  │ Reload grid data

examples.name: How Data Flows at Runtime Example 5
examples.code:
User is on /admin/actions/{id} (edit mode)
  │ Embedded list shows action evidence
  ▼
User clicks [+ Add Evidence] on embedded list
  │
  ▼
Router navigates to /admin/actions/{id}/evidence/new    ← child route
  │ Parent content hidden via CSS (d-none), NOT destroyed
  │ router-outlet activates child component
  ▼
Child component injects CaptureState
  │ Reads parent data (actionId, existing evidence for duplicate check)
  │ Generates temp UUID: private newRecordId = crypto.randomUUID()
  ▼
User fills child form → clicks [Save]
  │ Adds new item to state.evidence via state.evidence.update(list => [...list, newItem])
  │ newItem.id = this.newRecordId (temp client-side UUID)
  ▼
Router navigates back to /admin/actions/{id}
  │ Parent content shown again
  │ Embedded list reflects updated collection
  ▼
User clicks [Save] on PARENT capture page
  │ Parent maps child IDs: serverIds.has(id) ? id : EMPTY_GUID
  │ Sends full aggregate to API (parent + all children)

examples.name: What Does My Feature Need? Example 1
examples.code:
Is this a new admin feature?
├── YES → Models + Feature Service + List Page + Capture Page + Routes
└── NO  → Only the specific pieces changing

Does the entity have children shown as embedded lists?
├── YES → Capture State (component-scoped Injectable)
│         Embedded List component(s)
│         Child route components for add/edit
│         ID management (crypto.randomUUID → EMPTY_GUID on save)
└── NO  → Simple capture page with direct form fields

Does the capture page need child routes?
├── YES → Capture State (mandatory — shares data between parent and child routes)
│         Child route definitions (shared between /new and /:id)
│         Parent template: d-none wrapper + router-outlet
└── NO  → No capture state needed

Does the entity have lifecycle commands (activate, disable)?
├── YES → Domain command buttons (custom-buttons slot on toolbar)
│         Confirmation dialogs before destructive commands
│         Feature service methods for each command
└── NO  → Standard CRUD only

Does the list page need custom filters?
├── YES → Query parameters on route (?action=abc123)
│         Filter UI in toolbar
└── NO  → Built-in grid search is sufficient

examples.name: Implementation Order Example 1
examples.code:
1. Models — TypeScript interfaces mirroring API contracts
 2. Feature Service — HTTP methods (getGridList, get, save, delete)
 3. List Page — Grid with toolbar, selection, navigation
 4. Capture Page — Form with card sections, validation, save/cancel
 5. Capture State — Component-scoped state (only if aggregate with child routes)
 6. Embedded Lists — Child grids with add/edit/delete (only if aggregate)
 7. Child Route Components — Capture forms for child entities (only if child routes)
 8. Routing — Route definitions with lazy loading
 9. Validation — Per-field computed errors, isFormValid, duplicate detection

examples.code:
Capture page with NO child routes     → No capture state needed
Capture page with embedded list ONLY  → No capture state (component owns list directly)
Capture page with child routes         → Capture state REQUIRED

Layer 5: Capture State Example 6
examples.code:
Evidence-side                        API save
                    ──────────                         ────────
New child item  →  id = crypto.randomUUID()     →  id = EMPTY_GUID  →  server assigns Guid.CreateVersion7()
Existing item   →  id = server-assigned GUID    →  id = same GUID   →  server matches by ID → update
Removed item    →  absent from collection       →  absent from request  →  server detects via child diff → delete

examples.name: See [Embedded List](embedded-list.md) for full conventions.
Layer 6: Embedded Lists Example 2
examples.code:
Parent capture component
  │ owns signal<ChildDTO[]>
  │
  ├──▶ Embedded list component
  │     │ model() reads the collection
  │     │ model.update() modifies the collection
  │     │ Changes flow back to parent via two-way binding
  │
  └──▶ On parent Save:
        │ Maps child IDs (new → EMPTY_GUID)
        │ Sends full aggregate to API
        │ API command diffs children server-side

examples.name: See [Routing](routing.md) for full conventions.
See [Validation](validation.md) for full conventions.
examples.code:
Per-field errors (computed → string[])
  │ Required checks, length limits, format patterns
  │ Duplicate detection (server-loaded or sibling comparison)
  ▼
isFormValid (computed → boolean)
  │ All required fields non-empty
  │ All error arrays empty
  │ Not saving/loading
  ▼
Save button [disabled]="!isFormValid()"
  │
  ▼
API call
  │ Server-side validation (FluentValidation → 400)
  │ Business validation (command → exception)
  ▼
Error display
  │ API errors → inline alert (errorMessage signal)
  │ Field errors → [errors] binding on form components

Feature File Structure Example 1
examples.code:
modules/admin/{feature}/
├── {entity}-list.component.ts              # Grid page with toolbar
├── {entity}-list.component.html
├── {entity}-list.component.scss
├── {entity}-capture.component.ts           # Form page (create + edit)
├── {entity}-capture.component.html
├── {entity}-capture.component.scss
├── {entity}-capture.state.ts               # (only if child routes exist)
├── {entity}-{child}-list.component.ts      # (only if aggregate with children)
├── {entity}-{child}-list.component.html
├── {child}-capture.component.ts            # (only if child has its own route)
├── {child}-capture.component.html
├── {entity}.service.ts                     # HTTP service
└── {entity}.models.ts                      # TypeScript DTOs

rules.rule_text: Admin list pages must use nxgn-content, nxgn-grid-page, nxgn-grid-page-title, and nxgn-server-grid backed by a server-side grid-list endpoint.
Admin capture pages must use routed create/edit pages with nxgn-capture-page-title and feature-colocated services; aggregate roots must not be edited through modal CRUD.
Capture pages that need foreign-key options must load lookup data in parallel with aggregate data and submit one aggregate Save request through the feature service.
Menu labels, page titles, action labels, empty states, and validation summaries must consume seeded translation keys rather than hardcoded visible text.
Feature routes must be permission-guarded using the frontend permission constants that correspond to backend permission catalogue and role-template grants.
Do not implement client-side paging over a full table for admin catalogues; use nxgn-server-grid and the module grid-list endpoint.

sections.title: How Data Flows at Runtime
content: Before building anything, understand how user actions flow through the frontend layers:
Router activates WorkflowListComponent           ← standalone, OnPush, lazy-loaded
│ nxgn-data-grid (rows bound to dataItems(), columns to columnDefs)
User clicks [+ Add] on list page
│ Form signals initialized to defaults (empty strings, null selections)
│ Lookup data loaded (enum options for dropdowns)
│ Validation computed() functions recalculate in real-time
│ Slug auto-generated from Name (create mode only, stops on manual edit)
│ All form fields mapped to request shape
│ Child collections: temp UUIDs → EMPTY_GUID for new items
Router navigates to /admin/workflows/{id}        ← switches to edit mode
User double-clicks row on list page (or clicks Edit)
API returns WorkflowDTO (full entity with children)
│ state.steps.set(dto.steps)         ← capture state for child routes
│ Build SaveRequest (id = entity.id, steps mapped with ID management)
Reload entity data (re-fetch GET to sync server state)
User selects rows on list page → clicks [Delete]
### Child Route Flow (Aggregate with Embedded List)
User clicks [+ Add Evidence] on embedded list
│ Parent content hidden via CSS (d-none), NOT destroyed
│ Reads parent data (actionId, existing evidence for duplicate check)
│ Generates temp UUID: private newRecordId = crypto.randomUUID()
│ Adds new item to state.evidence via state.evidence.update(list => [...list, newItem])
User clicks [Save] on PARENT capture page
│ Parent maps child IDs: serverIds.has(id) ? id : EMPTY_GUID
│ Sends full aggregate to API (parent + all children)

sections.title: Admin Configuration Module Minimum Surface
content: A user-configurable admin module must provide a permission-gated frontend route, menu visibility, seeded translation keys, a server-grid list, and a routed aggregate capture page. The list uses NxGN grid components and server-side grid data. The capture page is the client-side aggregate root: it loads aggregate data and lookup data in parallel, owns editable signal state, validates before save, and submits one Save request through the feature service.

sections.title: What Does My Feature Need?
content: Ask these questions before adding web feature infrastructure:
1. **Does the route only show a grid/list?**
- Add a list page component and feature service.
2. **Does the route capture one aggregate on one page, including embedded child tables?**
- Add a routed capture component and a feature service.
- Keep the aggregate state inside the component unless multiple child routes need to coordinate.
3. **Does the feature have nested child routes that edit the same draft aggregate?**
- Use the capture state as the client-side aggregate root across child route components.
4. **Does the feature only need lookup data?**
- Use simple service methods or existing lookup helpers.
- Do not add capture state or a grid abstraction.
Embedded tables inside one routed capture page do not require capture state by themselves. Capture state is for multi-route aggregate editing, not for every form that contains child collections.

sections.title: Implementation Order
content: 1. Models — TypeScript interfaces mirroring API contracts
2. Feature Service — HTTP methods (getGridList, get, save, delete)
3. List Page — Grid with toolbar, selection, navigation
4. Capture Page — Form with card sections, validation, save/cancel
5. Capture State — Component-scoped state (only if aggregate with child routes)
6. Embedded Lists — Child grids with add/edit/delete (only if aggregate)
7. Child Route Components — Capture forms for child entities (only if child routes)
8. Routing — Route definitions with lazy loading
9. Validation — Per-field computed errors, isFormValid, duplicate detection

**Why this order?** Models define the shapes everything else references. The service provides the HTTP layer the components call. List page is the entry point users see first. Capture page is the primary interaction surface. State, embedded lists, and child routes layer on top for aggregates.

sections.title: Layer 1: Models
content: TypeScript interfaces mirroring the API contract types. All DTOs are `interface` (not `class`). One models file per feature.
// Grid item — flat, for list display
// Full DTO — complete entity shape (GET response)
// Save request — same shape as DTO (roundtrip pattern)
| **All DTOs are interfaces** | Not classes — no runtime instantiation overhead |
| **EnumDTO for classifications** | `{ id: number, name: string }` — binds to dropdowns directly |
| **NamedDTO for FK references** | `{ id: string, name: string, description?: string }` |
| **Grid item DTOs are flat** | Scalar fields only — `stepCount: number` not `steps: []` |
| **Save request matches DTO shape** | Zero-transformation roundtrip: GET → mutate → POST |
| **ID is always `string`** | GUIDs are strings in TypeScript — `EMPTY_GUID = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000'` |
| **One models file per feature** | `{entity}.models.ts` co-located with service and components |

sections.title: Layer 2: Feature Service
content: HTTP service co-located with the feature. One service per feature, `providedIn: 'root'` or feature-scoped. All methods return `Promise<T>` (via `firstValueFrom`).
getGridList(page: number, pageSize: number, search?: string): Promise<GridListResponse<WorkflowGridItemDTO>> {
| **One service per feature** | Co-located in feature folder ([ADR-0007](../../adr/0007-feature-colocated-services-on-web.md)) |
| **`inject()` for DI** | Functional injection, no constructor parameters |
| **`firstValueFrom`** | Converts Observable to Promise — async/await in components |
| **Save is upsert** | Single `save()` method for create and update |
| **Delete is POST** | `POST /delete` with `{ entityKeys }` body (aggregate pattern) |
| **Grid list is POST** | `POST /grid-list` with filter payload |
| **Lookup returns NamedDTO[]** | For dropdowns and autocomplete |

sections.title: Layer 3: List Page
content: Grid page with toolbar (Add/Edit/Delete), data grid with selection and search, and navigation to capture page.
import { Component, inject, signal, OnInit, ChangeDetectionStrategy } from '@angular/core';
private t(key: string, params?: Record<string, unknown>): string {
| **Selection** | `signal<{ id: string }[]>()` mapped from grid selection events |
| **Delete** | Confirmation dialog → API call → toast → reload grid |

sections.title: Layer 4: Capture Page
content: Form page for creating and editing entities. Same component handles both modes (`:id` route param determines mode). Card-based layout with form sections.
### How the capture page determines mode
Route: /admin/workflows/new    → id input = undefined → isEditMode = false → "New Workflow"
Route: /admin/workflows/{guid} → id input = guid      → isEditMode = true  → "Edit Workflow: {name}"
Component, inject, input, signal, computed, effect,
// Master form validity — gates Save button
// Effect: load entity when route param changes (edit mode)
// Effect: auto-generate slug from name (create mode only)
steps: [],  // simplified — see capture state for full step management
private t(key: string, params?: Record<string, unknown>): string {
| **Save flow** | Build request from signals → service.save() → navigate or reload |
| **Slug auto-gen** | Create mode only, stops on manual edit |
| **Error display** | Inline `alert alert-danger` for API errors |
| **Loading** | Spinner overlay, fields hidden during load |

sections.title: Layer 5: Capture State
content: Component-scoped `@Injectable()` that shares aggregate state between parent capture page and child route components. **Only needed when the capture page has child routes.**
Capture page with NO child routes     → No capture state needed
Capture page with embedded list ONLY  → No capture state (component owns list directly)
Capture page with child routes         → Capture state REQUIRED
@Injectable()  // NO providedIn — component-scoped, fresh per mount
providers: [ActionCaptureState],  // scoped to this component instance
// Track server-assigned IDs for new/existing detection on save
id: this.serverEvidenceIds.has(c.id) ? c.id : EMPTY_GUID,  // new items → EMPTY_GUID
<!-- Parent content hidden when child route active, NOT destroyed -->
<!-- capture page title, form cards, embedded lists -->
**Why CSS hide instead of `@if`?** Destroying the parent loses form state. CSS `d-none` keeps the component alive so edits survive child navigation.
// Generate temp ID once per mount
// Read sibling data for duplicate detection
New child item  →  id = crypto.randomUUID()     →  id = EMPTY_GUID  →  server assigns Guid.CreateVersion7()
Existing item   →  id = server-assigned GUID    →  id = same GUID   →  server matches by ID → update
Removed item    →  absent from collection       →  absent from request  →  server detects via child diff → delete

sections.title: Layer 6: Embedded Lists
content: Child grid shown inside a capture page, bound to a collection signal. Uses the same `nxgn-grid-page-title` + `nxgn-data-grid` components as list pages.
readonly model = model.required<WorkflowStepDTO[]>();  // two-way binding with parent
readonly disabled = input(false);  // read-only until parent is in edit mode
// OR for simple child entities, open inline form
│     │ Changes flow back to parent via two-way binding

sections.title: Layer 7: Routing
content: All admin features use lazy-loaded routes under `/admin` with `adminAuthGuard`. Three routes per entity for CRUD.
// Shared child route definitions — applied to both /new and /:id
| **Child routes** | Defined once, applied to both `/new` and `/:id` routes |
| **Input binding** | `withComponentInputBinding()` in app config — `:id` binds to `input()` |
| **No modals** | Always child routes for list → capture navigation ([ADR-0015](../../adr/0015-child-routes-over-modals-for-entity-capture.md)) |

sections.title: Layer 8: Validation
content: All validation is form-local using `computed()` error arrays. No external validation libraries. The `isFormValid` computed gates the Save button.
│ Required checks, length limits, format patterns
│ Field errors → [errors] binding on form components
// Duplicate detection (aggregate root — server-loaded unique names)
// Duplicate detection (child entity — sibling comparison via capture state)
| **isFormValid** | Single `computed()` checking all fields + UI state |
| **Duplicate detection** | Aggregate roots: server query; children: sibling comparison |

sections.title: Feature File Structure
content: ├── {child}-capture.component.ts            # (only if child has its own route)

sections.title: Complete Feature Checklist
content: - [ ] Full entity DTO (with children if aggregate)
- [ ] Save request interface (same shape as DTO for roundtrip)
- [ ] EnumDTO / NamedDTO for classification and FK fields
- [ ] Domain command methods (if lifecycle operations exist)
- [ ] Form signals: one per editable field
- [ ] Effect: load entity on route param change (edit mode)
- [ ] Effect: auto-generate slug from name (create mode, if applicable)
- [ ] Save: build request → service.save() → navigate (create) or reload (edit)
- [ ] ID management: EMPTY_GUID for create, entity ID for update
- [ ] Signals for parent ID + all child collections
- [ ] Server ID tracking for new/existing detection
- [ ] Child routes: shared definition applied to both `/new` and `/:id`
- [ ] `inject()` for all service injection (no constructor params)
